title: Secretary Statements & Remarks | U.S. Department of the Treasury
summary: This is a navigation/listing page providing titles of Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's remarks, speeches, and congressional testimony from December 2025 through March 2026. No substantive policy content is available in this source; it merely indexes individual documents. Actual archival content would require visiting each linked speech or statement page.
key_points: Source is a listing page containing titles of 11 remarks by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent
Dated materials range from December 17, 2025 to March 30, 2026
Includes congressional testimony before Senate Banking Committee (Feb 4, 2026) and House Financial Services Committee (Jan 28, 2026)
Covers topics including liquidity regulation, economic security, financial literacy, and Trump Accounts policy
No substantive policy content or quotes provided in this index page
why_it_matters: This source serves only as an index to Treasury Secretary remarks and cannot be archived as a standalone research document. Individual speech pages would need to be crawled separately for substantive policy content relevant to market structure or macro catalysts.

issues_found: Source is a listing_page per source metadata, violating Rule 6 (reject homepage, navigation_page, listing_page, search_page)
No substantive policy content available - only document titles and dates
event_type is 'unknown' because no actual speech or testimony content exists to classify
Cannot assess market impact, macro_context, or market_structure_context without accessing individual linked documents
Archival value is zero as a standalone record - individual speeches/testimony would need to be crawled separately

notes: This is a low-quality source for archival purposes. It provides only titles of Treasury Secretary remarks without substantive content. Rule 6 applies: listing pages are non-archiveable. Individual linked documents (speeches, testimony, statements) should be crawled separately to obtain archiveable content. The congressional testimony before Senate Banking (Feb 4) and House Financial Services (Jan 28) committees may be most relevant to macro catalysts.
